reason: Bitcoin is experiencing a markup phase, driven by significant institutional inflows into BTC ETFs and its perceived safe-haven status amid geopolitical volatility. While this fuels upward momentum, the broader market remains sensitive to regulatory developments, as seen with the proposed CBDC ban, and sector-specific weaknesses (Core Scientific). These factors introduce a higher degree of risk and potential for sudden shifts, despite the current bullish sentiment.
headlines: Bitcoin climbs as BTC ETFs post one of the quarter’s biggest inflow days amid Iran volatility
NEAR token jumps 17% after ‘Confidential Intents’ launch, outpaces privacy tokens sector
U.S. Senate housing bill includes CBDC ban
PayPay, 40% owner of Binance Japan, seeks up to $1.1 billion in Nasdaq IPO
Core Scientific turns lower after Q4 results disappoint
